description_long: This is a small, single-propeller airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a low-wing configuration. The body is primarily painted in a light blue color, with a slightly raised cockpit canopy situated near the front of the fuselage. The wings are straight and extend horizontally from the lower part of the fuselage, with no visible winglets or dihedral angle. The tail section features a conventional vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizer, forming a classic tail design. The landing gear appears to be a taildragger configuration, with a small wheel at the rear and main gear under the wings. The propeller is mounted at the nose, and the cockpit is enclosed with a single canopy window.
description: A small, light blue, single-propeller airplane with a low-wing and conventional tail design. It features an enclosed cockpit and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the streamlined fuselage, low-mounted straight wings, and the propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the horizontal and vertical stabilizers, as well as the full wingspan.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the taildragger gear and cockpit canopy.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the propeller, nose, and symmetrical wing placement.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight wings with no visible winglets or dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main wheels and a single rear tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed single-seat cockpit with a clear canopy.
